message:
test(abi): add faccessat2 + io_pgetevents + clone3 ABI coverage

Companion test coverage for the three ABI shims added in the previous
commit. Each test pair exercises the success-path-or-known-errno
contract that the ABI shim has to satisfy across kernel versions.

faccessat2_existing_file_returns_zero
  - Creates a real file in std::env::temp_dir() (PID-suffixed for
    parallel safety), confirms faccessat2 returns 0 on a modern
    kernel and accepts ENOSYS on pre-5.8 kernels via the ABI errno
    bridge.

faccessat2_missing_file_returns_enoent
  - faccessat2 against a non-existent path must produce -1 with
    errno ∈ {ENOENT, ENOSYS} — proving both that the kernel error
    is propagated and that the path argument is actually consulted.

io_pgetevents_invalid_ctx_returns_einval
  - ctx_id = 0 is never a valid io_setup() handle. Verify
    io_pgetevents returns -1 with errno ∈ {EINVAL, ENOSYS}, so
    callers see a clean rejection rather than UB.

io_pgetevents_null_events_with_positive_nr_returns_efault
  - NULL events buffer with nr > 0 must return -1 with EFAULT (or
    ENOSYS). Pins the kernel-side pointer-validation contract so a
    future ABI refactor cannot start silently dropping events on
    NULL pointers.

(plus the two new clone3 cases that complete the file's coverage).

All tests use the standard ABI errno location lookup
(__errno_location()) and tolerate ENOSYS as a portable fallback,
matching the surrounding test conventions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
